commit.message:
fix: make Phase 2 dependency guard blocking with -Force override

Changed deploy-agent-flows.ps1 Phase 2 guard from Write-Warning
(non-blocking) to throw (blocking). Deploying Phase 2 without Phase 1
now fails immediately with a clear message. Use -Force to override.

Also verified (all clean):
- Zero hardcoded GUIDs across all 15 flow JSONs
- All entityName refs use correct plural forms (cr_followuptrackings,
  cr_nudgeconfigurations, cr_snoozedconversations, systemusers)
- Security role grants Basic (user-level) depth only — CRUD + Append/
  AppendTo on 3 tables, no org-level or admin privileges

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add BQL executor — full parse→compile→execute pipeline

New query/execute.rs: runs compiled SQL against SQLite index.

Features:
- QueryResult enum: Rows, Count, GroupCounts
- Typed CellValue: Text, Int, Bool, Null
- $page variable resolved at runtime (empty string → active page ID)
- run_query() convenience: parse → compile → execute in one call
- 12 integration tests against in-memory SQLite with test data

Full BQL pipeline operational:
  'tasks | where not done and due < today | sort due | limit 10'
  → tokenise → parse AST → compile SQL → execute → typed rows

67 BQL tests total (33 parser + 22 compiler + 12 executor).
394 total workspace tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: fix Linux perf data collection + add avg AVX2 speedup to README

- Fix PERF_REPORT_DIR: change /output to /project/tests in both Linux
  package jobs (arm64 + x64). In cibuildwheel v3, /output is not reliably
  accessible from the test container; /project maps directly to the host
  source tree which is writable. Remove now-unnecessary cp wheelhouse/
  perf_report.* steps (confirmed no-op in CI logs).

- Add average AVX2 speedup to README: combine_perf_reports.py now computes
  the geometric mean speedup across all x86_64 platforms and instances at
  markers. Add the markers to README.md.

commit.message:
Fix migration hang at 83% during re-migrations

Critical fixes for headless migration stalling:

1. Mark CSS phase as 100% complete (async-migration-runner.php)
   - Added missing update_progress() call after global CSS migration
   - Allows phase to transition properly instead of hanging at 75%

2. Clear processed_post_ids from old migrations (async-migration-runner.php)
   - Explicitly set processed_post_ids and processed_media_ids to empty arrays
   - Prevents re-migrations from inheriting 'already processed' IDs from prior runs
   - Fixes 0/480 display and allows templates to be re-processed

3. Safety guard for stale processed IDs (batch-phase-runner.php)
   - If processed_count=0 (new run), clear any stale processed_ids from checkpoint
   - Prevents filtering out all remaining IDs due to idempotency check

Root cause: Re-migrations loaded checkpoints containing processed_ids from
previous migrations. New batch run would have processed_count=0 but
processed_ids_set={480 IDs from prior run}, so ALL remaining IDs were filtered
out as 'already processed'. Batch loop had nothing to do, phase never completed.

This prevents the infinite re-enqueue cycle where Action Scheduler would keep
retrying the same stuck state without making progress.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(tools): Sprint A Wave 2 -- scene checker, project validator, integration gate

Closes #33
Closes #35
Closes #37

Built 3 critical automation tools for M3 integration discipline:

Tool 5: Scene Integrity Checker (check-scenes.py)
- Scans all .tscn files in games/ashfall/scenes/
- Validates script references exist on disk
- Checks collision_layer/mask against expected scheme
- Reports PASS/FAIL with line numbers

Tool 6: Godot Headless Validator (validate-project.py)
- Validates project.godot structure
- Checks [autoload] files exist
- Validates [input] actions are well-formed
- Verifies main_scene exists
- Runs autoload checker and signal validator as sub-checks
- Comprehensive project health report

Tool 7: Integration Gate Automation (integration-gate.py + workflow)
- Runs ALL validators in sequence
- Reports overall PASS/WARN/FAIL
- GitHub Action on push to main
- Auto-creates issues on failure with details
- This is the ceremony that prevents dead code syndrome

All tools tested and working. Integration gate passed all checks.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add BQL SQL compiler

New query/compile.rs: AST → SQL with parameterised bind values.

Features:
- Field validation per source (unknown field → compile error)
- Type checking (has only on tags, ranges only on dates)
- Operators: =, !=, <, >, <=, >= → direct SQL mapping
- has #tag → subquery: IN (SELECT page_id FROM tags WHERE tag = ?)
- Ranges (this week, last month, etc.) → date range with chrono
- none → IS NULL / IS NOT NULL
- backlinks.count → correlated subquery
- $page, $today → parameterised bind values
- count → wraps in SELECT COUNT(*)
- group + count → per-group counts via GROUP BY

22 compiler tests + 33 parser tests = 55 BQL tests.
382 total workspace tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Backtesting & strategy comparison UI (#207)

* Initial plan

* feat: implement backtesting and strategy comparison UI

- Add Strategy types (StrategyResponse, CreateStrategyRequest) to types.ts
- Add Backtest types (BacktestRunResponse, RunBacktestRequest, etc.) to types.ts
- Create strategiesApi service with list/getById/create/delete
- Create backtestsApi service with list/getById/run/delete
- Create useStrategies and useBacktests React Query hooks
- Create StrategyCard component with delete confirmation
- Create CreateStrategyForm with dynamic parameters per strategy type
  (BUY_AND_HOLD, DOLLAR_COST_AVERAGING, MOVING_AVERAGE_CROSSOVER)
- Create RunBacktestForm with validation (date range, initial cash)
- Create BacktestMetrics component showing key metrics in card grid
- Create ComparisonChart for normalized % return overlay using Recharts
- Create ComparisonTable with best/worst coloring per metric
- Create Strategies page (/strategies) with grid layout
- Create Backtests page (/backtests) with table, checkboxes, compare button
- Create BacktestResult page (/backtests/:id) with metrics + performance chart
- Create CompareBacktests page (/compare?ids=...) using useQueries for parallel loading
- Add navigation tabs to App.tsx (Portfolios, Strategies, Backtests)
- Add routes for /strategies, /backtests, /backtests/:id, /compare
- Add unit tests for strategiesApi, backtestsApi, StrategyCard, ComparisonTable

All 32 test files pass, TypeScript compiles cleanly, ESLint has no errors.

* fix: address code review feedback on ComparisonTable and RunBacktestForm

- ComparisonTable: simplify getReturnHighlights with clear higher/lower logic
  and fix max drawdown highlighting (higher = closer to 0 = better, use true)
- RunBacktestForm: compute both start/end dates from same Date instance
  to avoid midnight edge case inconsistency

* feat: implement backtesting & strategy comparison UI

Co-authored-by: TimChild <48571510+TimChild@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add glx places command for place standardization analysis (#49)

* feat: add glx places command for place standardization analysis

Analyzes places for ambiguity and completeness: flags duplicate names,
missing coordinates, missing types, hierarchy gaps, and unreferenced
places. Shows canonical hierarchy paths for each place.

* fix: address Copilot review on places command

- Normalize whitespace and skip empty names in duplicate detection
- Add dangling parent detection (parent ID references missing place)
- Add single-file archive test for loadArchiveForPlaces coverage
- Use t.TempDir() instead of hard-coded path in nonexistent test

* fix: address Copilot review feedback on places command

- Update help text to match actual unreferenced detection behavior
- Trim and skip empty place names in buildCanonicalPath
- Display original name casing in duplicate groups instead of normalized
- Include assertion subject.place references in unreferenced detection

* fix: warn on duplicate entity IDs, fix double-wrapped error, add missing type and assertion-ref tests

* fix: use correct type glxlib.EntityRef in assertion test

* fix: add nil guards for places, events, and assertions to prevent panics on malformed archives

* fix: handle nil parent entries in dangling detection, parent references, and duplicate cleanup

* fix: remove stray conflict marker from cli_commands.go

* bump version

* bump version

* fix CLAUDE.md

commit.message:
Add molecule tests for reverse-proxy role

Validate template rendering for Caddyfile, compose.caddy.yml, and

commit.message:
feat: add onListModels handler to CopilotClientOptions for BYOK mode (#730)

Add an optional onListModels handler to CopilotClientOptions across all
4 SDKs (Node, Python, Go, .NET). When provided, client.listModels()
calls the handler instead of sending the models.list RPC to the CLI
server. This enables BYOK users to return their provider's available
models in the standard ModelInfo format.

- Handler completely replaces CLI RPC when set (no fallback)
- Results cached identically to CLI path (same locking/thread-safety)
- No connection required when handler is provided
- Supports both sync and async handlers
- 10 new unit tests across all SDKs
- Updated BYOK docs with usage examples in all 4 languages

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix async message channel errors on page unload/navigation

- Add DOM existence checks after each async operation in autoResumeMigration()
  to prevent listener errors when component unmounts before response completes
- Check document.querySelector('[data-efs-bricks-wizard]') after each await
  to detect if wizard was removed from DOM
- Add beforeunload handler to ensure clean shutdown if user navigates away
  while async operations are in flight

Prevents 'listener indicated asynchronous response by returning true,
but the message channel closed before a response was received' errors

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix MenuOptionsList thread-safety: build into local list, atomic swap (#1303)

BuildAppInfoMenuOptionsAsync previously called .Clear() on the shared
MenuOptionsList and then mutated it in-place. Under concurrent requests
this is a data race — readers could see a partially-built or empty list.

Fix: build into a local List<IMenuOption>, then assign to the property
in a single reference swap. Readers always see a complete, consistent
menu.

Fixes #1303

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: modular monolith architecture with 8 projects

Restructure monolithic BoardGame.API into modular monolith:

- BoardGame.Shared.Kernel: contracts, interfaces, domain events
- BoardGame.Infrastructure: services, middleware (auth, rate-limit, validation)
- BoardGame.Modules.Identity: auth + configuration (AppConfig)
- BoardGame.Modules.Characters: characters + character sheets
- BoardGame.Modules.Sessions: sessions + notes
- BoardGame.Modules.Gallery: gallery + image generation + templates
- BoardGame.Modules.Live: live sessions, chat, cinema, soundboard, SignalR hub
- BoardGame.Host: composition root wiring all modules

Each module has its own DbContext mapping only its tables.
Cross-module references use plain int IDs without navigation properties.
Domain events defined via MemoryEventBus.Abstractions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add /voice/stream SSE endpoint for voice progress

Stream progress events during voice AI processing before sending

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add BQL tokeniser and parser (Phase 1)

New query/ module with tokeniser and recursive descent parser:

Tokeniser: keywords, operators, strings, dates, numbers, tags (#rust),
variables ($page), ranges (this week, last month), dotted fields.
Position tracking for error diagnostics.

Parser: QUERY = SOURCE (| CLAUSE)*
- Sources: pages, tasks, journal, blocks, tags, links
- Clauses: where (and/or/not/parens), sort, group, limit, count
- Predicates: field OP value, field has #tag, field RANGE, bare boolean
- No double negation (parse error)
- count must be last clause
- Keywords accepted as field names (tags, count, date, etc.)

33 tests covering tokenisation, parsing, errors, and complex queries.
All 364 workspace tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: implement Workflow Planner for capability graph (#1260)

Add WorkflowPlanner that generates executable multi-step workflow plans
from natural language intent by parsing user statements, mapping them to
capability graph nodes, and validating step reachability via BFS.

New files:
- WorkflowPlan.cs: Plan types (WorkflowStep, StepType, WorkflowPlan)
  with output variable chaining and validation error tracking
- WorkflowPlanner.cs: NL parser with verb classification, entity
  matching (plural/singular), condition extraction, action inference,
  and graph reachability validation

Features:
- Splits input into statements (newlines, periods, semicolons)
- Classifies verbs → StepType (query/create/update/delete/run-action)
- Matches entities by name, slug, or plural form against graph
- Extracts conditions after marker words (who/where/that/with/etc.)
- Infers action names from graph or input tokens
- Chains output variables as downstream step inputs
- Validates reachability between consecutive steps via bounded BFS
- Human-readable plan preview with emoji step icons

Integration:
- POST /api/admin/workflow-plan endpoint returns structured JSON plan
- plan-workflow intent registered in AdminToolCatalogue
- Accessible via admin chat: "plan workflow: find customers..."

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: cap cloud futures to prevent GPU phase starvation

During GPU phases, _submit_io_jobs claimed unlimited cloud_ai jobs into a
1-thread ThreadPoolExecutor. With copilot (1 worker, ~10s/job), thousands
of futures accumulated. When the phase ended, ThreadPoolExecutor.shutdown()
blocked for hours waiting for the backlog, preventing blip2/faces/embedding
from advancing to the next phase.

Fix:
- Track _cloud_in_flight counter in _submit_io_jobs
- Cap to effective_cloud_workers * 2 (2 for copilot)
- Decrement in _collect_futures and _cancel_futures
- Cancel uncompleted futures at phase boundaries so the pool shuts down
  promptly; cancelled jobs return to pending for the IO drain phase

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add Nord Pool price-optimized charging schedule

Fetches hourly prices from Nord Pool API (cached 1h), selects the
cheapest hours within the charging window to concentrate charging.

Algorithm:
- Calculate hours needed at minimum rate (6A) with 20% buffer
- Rank remaining hours by price, pick cheapest N
- If current hour is cheap → charge at deadline rate
- If current hour is expensive AND charger hasn't started → wait
- If charger already running during expensive hour → continue at min rate
- Fallback: if API unavailable, reverts to spread-evenly strategy

Example: 5 kWh needed over 8h window → picks cheapest 2h out of 8,
charges during 02:00-04:00 (0.65 SEK/kWh) instead of 22:00 (0.83 SEK)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  sync-settings-canvas-app-source.ps1

  (prevents broken images in network-restricted tenants)

- Pin requirements.txt upper bounds (rich<15, requests<3, msal<2)
- Add network prerequisite note to demo-walkthrough.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

build: reduce wheel size via OpenBLAS DYNAMIC_LIST + NO_CBLAS + strip

- Add DYNAMIC_LIST to OpenBLAS builds: generic uses NEHALEM..ZEN3
  (drops pre-2010 arches like BARCELONA/CORE2); AVX2 uses HASWELL..ZEN3.
  CPUs not in the list fall back to the generic kernel automatically.
- Add NO_CBLAS=1: COIN-OR calls Fortran-style BLAS (dgemm_ etc.) so
  the cblas_* C wrappers are dead weight.
- Add _strip_binaries(): runs strip --strip-unneeded (Linux) / strip -x
  (macOS) on all shared libs and executables after each release build.
  Debug builds are intentionally not stripped.

Expected savings: ~30-40% off OpenBLAS, ~30% off COIN-OR libs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
